column1: Trading is not about proving how smart you are, but how disciplined you are.
Money is made by sitting, not trading.
The market will do move to frustrate the majority.
Successful traders don't seek excitement; they seek probability.
If you cannot control your emotions, you cannot control your money.
Professional traders focus on keeping money; amateurs focus on making it.
The biggest enemy is within yourself.
Trading is like hunting; if you're too tired, you can't pull the trigger.
Discipline is the foundation of successful trading.
Fear and greed are the two shackles of a trader.
Cut your losses and let your profits run.
Never add to a losing position.
Survival is the only thing that matters.
Before you enter, know how much you can lose.
Stop loss is your only insurance.
Take risks only when you can afford the consequences.
Money management is the secret to success.
Don't put all your eggs in one basket.
A small loss prevents a large loss.
Guard your capital like your life.
The trend is your friend.
Trade with the trend, stop against it.
The market is always right.
Don't fight the market.
Volume precedes price.
Buy strength, sell weakness.
History repeats itself.
Simplicity is beauty in trading.
Focus on price action, not the news.
Wait for the best entry point.
The market reflects all known information.
Bull markets are born on pessimism.
Price is determined by fear and greed.
The stock market is a device for transferring money from the impatient to the patient.
There are no good or bad markets, only up and down.
Buy stocks that are going up and sell stocks that are going down.
The market is like an elephant; you can't tell it where to go, you can only follow it.
If you find yourself in a hole, the first thing to do is stop digging.
Everything is reflected in price movements.
Trading is not a hobby; it’s a serious business.
Better to miss a trade than to make a mistake.
Never buy a stock because it has a big decline from its previous high.
The best trades are often the ones that are hardest to take.
The market is designed to fool most of the people, most of the time.
Half of trading success is knowing yourself.
Don't chase the market; let the market come to you.
If you can't afford to lose, don't play.
Profits take care of themselves; losses never do.
Risk is not gambling unless you don’t know what you are doing.
Every trade should have a risk-to-reward ratio.
Profit is the byproduct of patient waiting.
Never let a winning trade turn into a losing one.
Losing is not shameful; letting losses grow is.
When in doubt, get out.
Preservation of capital is the first rule of trading.
The market can remain irrational longer than you can remain solvent.
Trading is not about truth; it's about profit.
Every day is a new day; don't trade with yesterday's emotions.
You don't have to trade every day unless you have a good reason.
Successful people learn from their failures.
Only fools try to capture the last penny.
Make your system work for you, not you for the system.
Real wealth is accumulated through patience.
Stocks have no feelings; don't fall in love with them.
Keep it simple, stupid (KISS).
Prediction is low-level; reaction is high-level.
The market is a combination of human nature and numbers.
Confidence comes from your backup plan.
Don’t try to catch a falling knife.
The stop loss is your best friend.
Trading should be as natural as breathing.
It's all about probabilities.
Winners take responsibility; losers blame the market.
A clear head is more important than any technique.
Resting is part of the trade.
Knowing when not to trade is the true mastery.
Discipline must be practiced every day.
Failure is part of the cost of doing business.
Trust your intuition, but base it on experience.
The market will tell you when you're wrong.
Focus on the process, not the outcome.
Stay humble before the market.
Mistakes are the best teachers.
Imitation leads to mediocrity; thinking leads to excellence.
Don't trade for the sake of trading.
Trading is a lonely journey.
Your system is your faith.
All greatness comes from small accumulations.
Conquer yourself before conquering the market.
The market always rewards the prepared.
Survive long, earn more.
The market owes you nothing.
All wealth is hidden in risk.
Stay hungry, stay humble.
Learn to live with uncertainty.
Trading is both art and science.
Every trade is a test of character.
The market is the truest reflection of human nature.
Don't let a single failure define you.
Trading is not your whole life.
